title: hieracium murorum subsp. lingua
intro: Hieracium murorum subsp. lingua, also known as a type of hawkweed, is a perennial plant species in the dandelion family, characterized by its yellow or orange flowers and edible leaves. The medicinal properties of Hieracium murorum subsp. lingua include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, which are attributed to the plant's high content of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of consuming Hieracium murorum subsp. lingua include its ability to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing, making it a valuable addition to herbal remedies. Hieracium murorum subsp. lingua can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties and provide a natural remedy for various health issues.
